description:
Using ~/Fiji_Data/raw/confocal/FluorescentCells.tif (3-channel fluorescence image, 512×512, pixel size 0.32 µm), perform per-channel quantitative analysis and assemble a publication figure.

Split the image into its three channels and calibrate each at 0.32 µm/pixel.

For the blue channel (nuclei): apply flat-field correction using Gaussian blur division (sigma=50), then background subtraction (rolling ball 50px), Otsu thresholding with watershed separation, and particle analysis (100–5000 px², circularity 0.4–1.0, excluding edges). Record total cell count and mean nuclear area.

For the green channel (microtubules): apply background subtraction (rolling ball 30px), auto-threshold, skeletonize the binary image, and run skeleton analysis. Record total branch count and junction count.

For the red channel (F-actin): apply background subtraction (rolling ball 20px), auto-threshold, and particle analysis (20–2000 px², circularity 0.0–1.0, excluding edges). Record actin feature count and mean feature area.

Create an RGB composite by merging the three channels to their respective colors. Assemble a 2×3 publication montage showing: [Blue ch | Green ch | Red ch | Nuclear mask | Skeleton map | RGB composite] with a 10 µm scale bar.

Export channel_measurements.csv with columns Channel, Metric_Name, Value (minimum 6 rows: nuclear_count, mean_nuclear_area_um2, branch_count, junction_count, actin_feature_count, mean_actin_feature_area_um2), and analysis_report.txt summarizing all metrics with units. All outputs to ~/Fiji_Data/results/cell_pipeline/.
